differences.title: 1. PERFORMANCE
differences.leftDesc: Bun offers superior speed for script execution and bundling. Significantly faster.
differences.rightDesc: Node.js is performant but generally slower than Bun for many tasks.

differences.title: 2. INTEGRATION
differences.leftDesc: Bun includes bundler, transpiler, and package manager out-of-the-box. Minimal setup.
differences.rightDesc: Node.js relies on separate tools like Webpack/Vite and npm/Yarn. Extensive ecosystem.

differences.title: 3. COMPATIBILITY
differences.leftDesc: Bun natively supports modern ECMAScript modules and TypeScript.
differences.rightDesc: Node.js has strong support for CommonJS modules, with ES module support evolving.

differences.title: 4. PACKAGE MANAGER
differences.leftDesc: Bun has its own built-in, fast package manager (`bun install`).
differences.rightDesc: Node.js uses npm or Yarn, which are separate, well-established package managers.

differences.title: 5. API
differences.leftDesc: Bun implements Web APIs (like Fetch) for consistency across environments.
differences.rightDesc: Node.js has its own set of APIs (e.g., `fs` module).

differences.title: 6. STABILITY
differences.leftDesc: Bun is newer, rapidly developing, but may have breaking changes.
differences.rightDesc: Node.js is mature, battle-tested, and extremely stable for production.

differences.title: 7. COMMUNITY
differences.leftDesc: Bun has a passionate and rapidly expanding community.
differences.rightDesc: Node.js boasts one of the largest and most established developer communities.
